title: Translating a Point on the Coordinate Plane

steps.instruction: A translation slides a figure without rotating or flipping it.
steps.work: Every point moves the same distance in the same direction.
steps.explanation: Translation preserves shape and size — it just changes position.

steps.instruction: Translate point (3, 5) by 4 units left and 2 units down.
steps.explanation: Left subtracts from x. Down subtracts from y.

steps.instruction: Check: the new point is 4 units left and 2 units down from the original.
steps.work: Original (3, 5) → New (−1, 3). Difference: x changed by −4, y changed by −2.
steps.explanation: The translation rule (x − 4, y − 2) was applied correctly.

title: Reflecting a Point Over an Axis

steps.instruction: Reflecting over the x-axis flips the point vertically. The x-coordinate stays the same; the y-coordinate changes sign.
steps.explanation: The point is the same horizontal distance from the y-axis, but on the opposite side of the x-axis.

steps.instruction: Reflecting over the y-axis flips the point horizontally. The y-coordinate stays the same; the x-coordinate changes sign.
steps.explanation: The point is the same vertical height, but on the opposite side of the y-axis.

steps.work: Over x-axis: (x, y) → (x, −y). Over y-axis: (x, y) → (−x, y).
steps.explanation: The axis you reflect over stays unchanged; the other coordinate flips sign.
